findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `AbelJacobiSum.lean:198: change S.degreeCorrection w h x₀ (S.divisorClass D) =`; the only exposed splitting formula is `DegreeSplitting.lean:220-224 classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt_apply`, so this proof is depending on the exact product/subtype shape.
findings.fix: Replace the `change` with a rewrite through a named component/coercion lemma, or add a short comment explaining why this defeq is the intended stable interface here.
findings.issue: `classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt_divisorClass` uses `change` after `Prod.ext`/`Subtype.ext` to identify the first component through definitional equality of the splitting wrapper.

findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `AbelJacobiSum.lean:352: change S.degreeCorrection (fun _ : X => (1 : ℤ)) h x₀ (S.divisorClass D) =`; this is the unweighted analogue of the brittle wrapper-defeq proof at line 198.
findings.fix: Use an explicit weighted-to-unweighted component lemma/rewrite, or document why the definitional equality through the specialization `(fun _ => 1)` is unavoidable and stable.
findings.issue: `classGroupAddEquivUnweightedPicZeroProdInt_divisorClass` repeats the same undocumented `change` across the unweighted splitting wrapper.

summary: The proofs are mostly straightforward, but two splitting-component proofs rely on undocumented `change` steps across subtype/product wrappers. Per the proof-quality rubric, these should either be avoided with explicit lemmas/rewrites or documented if the definitional equality is intentional.
